Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
John Denver’s “Take Me Home, Country Roads” is enjoying a new wave of popularity as its sing-along chorus becomes a staple among soccer fans nationwide.

U.S. Soccer officials selected the song as the postmatch celebration tune for the men’s national team. Fans have since delighted in the resurgence of the classic 1971 country-folk hit, with videos circulating across social media of World Cup viewers proudly belting the lyrics.
On Friday, after the United States’ 2-0 win over Australia, the Seattle stadium of nearly 67,000 attendees broke out into a booming rendition of the song.

“The atmosphere was insane,” U.S. men’s national team midfielder Sebastian Berhalter said in an article on the U.S. Soccer website. “The chanting, the excitement, the energy — we felt that the whole game ... them singing ‘Country Roads’ was pretty special.”

A spokesperson for U.S. Soccer said FIFA had asked teams to submit potential playlists for warmups, goals and wins. When trying to determine suitable postgame songs for wins, the team sought ones that were “both representative of American artists and would be great sing-alongs for the crowd.”
“Take Me Home, Country Roads” and “Livin’ on a Prayer” were the winners. The shortlist also included “Sweet Caroline,” according to the spokesperson, but England had already used that song.

Over the past half-century, the lyrics, “Country roads, take me home / To the place I belong / West Virginia, mountain mama / Take me home, country roads,” have become recognizable even beyond U.S. borders, as the song has cemented itself as a wildly popular tune around the world.
The World Cup, which is hosted by the U.S., Canada and Mexico, has turned host cities into hubs of cultural exchange as fans arrive from around the world.
Americans expressed a renewed sense of pride over their cultural staples, including Buc-ee’s and ranch dip, when tourists discovered them for the first time.
And for many immigrants and children of immigrants, seeing the World Cup in the United States brought them a rare opportunity to celebrate two home countries in one place.
Several World Cup viewers online said they feel singing “Country Roads” has become a way to express patriotism, support the national team and connect with fellow soccer fans worldwide.

In Atlanta, attendees at Czechia’s match against South Africa on Thursday and Spain’s match against Saudi Arabia on Sunday were seen singing the chorus’s lyrics even after the track stopped playing. One video filmed in Boston shows a crowd of Scotland fans clapping and singing along.

“Look, I love John Denver (rest his soul), but if you had told me that the entire world, apparently, knows ‘Take Me Home, Country Roads’--let alone that it would be a certified World Cup banger--I definitely would not have believed you,” writer and activist Charlotte Clymer wrote in a social media post.

“I think it’s because we all want to go back to when the world made sense—now more prominent because we live in unrecognizable times. The ache of returning to something that felt safe is understandable. We are homesick,” an X user noted in their post. “In many ways, the World Cup in the USA has given people the freedom to feel what they needed: frivolity, kinship, and escape from stupid politics.”
But the song’s political undertones also sparked debate among some.

“Five years ago, professional athletes were wearing BLM masks on the field. Now, packed stadiums are singing ‘Country Roads’ after Team USA advances in the World Cup,” Charles Downs, a reporter for Laura Loomer’s far-right news show, wrote in a social media post. “The revolution is actually being televised. Welcome to the Golden Age.”

In response, several online pointed out that Denver was an outspoken left-leaning social and environmental activist until his death in 1997. His estate has previously spoken against the politicization of the song.

In 2016, after then-presidential candidate Donald Trump played “Take Me Home, Country Roads” at a rally in West Virginia, Denver’s estate shared a statement online requesting “that all candidates refrain from using his music.”

“We appreciate the admiration recently expressed for John Denver and his music,” the estate wrote at the time, “and believe that all candidates can benefit from his environmental and humanitarian legacy and from his commitment for peace.”

title: From The Sports Desk: What will Messi, Mbappé and Haaland do for an encore?
summary: Last week, Argentina’s Lionel Messi, France’s Kylian Mbappé and Norway’s Erling Haaland scored a combined seven goals on the same day.
story: Last week, Argentina’s Lionel Messi, France’s Kylian Mbappé and Norway’s Erling Haaland scored a combined seven goals on the same day. They are all in action again today. Call in sick to work, cancel your plans and turn on Peacock.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
This might be one of the best days of the tournament. The NBC News team will be live-blogging all the games, and we’ll make sure you don’t miss a thing.
World Cup Recap

Mohamed Salah has finally made his mark at the World Cup, scoring and assisting in Egypt’s 3-1 win over New Zealand, his nation’s first win in the competition. The Pharaohs moved to the top of the standings in Group G with the win but are still not yet assured a spot in the knockout round. They play a decider against Iran on Friday in Seattle, needing at least a draw to advance.

Iran, too, can still advance to the knockout round — something it has never done in six previous World Cup appearances, dating back to 1978. It registered a second point of the competition in a goalless draw against Belgium, which had 23 shots on goal but had Nathan Ngoy sent off in the second half and had to finish the game with 10 players.

Cape Verde followed up their surprise draw against Spain by holding two-time champions Uruguay to a 2-2 draw in Miami. Spain trounced Saudi Arabia 4-0, Lamine Yamal becoming only the second player 18 or younger to open the scoring in a FIFA World Cup match, following in the footsteps of Pelé in 1958.
World Cup Preview
If you only tune in for one day of group stage action, perhaps make it today: Messi surpassed Germany’s Miroslav Klose’s 16 goals to become the World Cup’s all-time leading scorer.
Mbappé of France, Messi’s former teammate at Paris Saint-Germain, has 14 goals, having bagged a brace against Senegal in Group I, and could himself surpass the record with a hat trick against Iraq.
Like Mbappé, Norway’s Haaland already has two goals in the World Cup. He’ll be aiming to add to that tally against Senegal later today.
Men In Blazers

Reigning European champions Spain finally looked like tournament favorites, with teenage star Lamine Yamal leading them to a 4-0 pummeling of Saudi Arabia. Manager Luis de la Fuente compared Barcelona’s artist-in-residence to Salvador Dali and Michelangelo, but could either of those men score 10 minutes into their first World Cup start?

Meanwhile, the side that stopped La Roja in their tracks last week, Cape Verde, has stunningly kept their World Cup dream alive. The Blue Sharks drew Uruguay 2-2, with La Celeste goalkeeper Fernando Muslera dropping a stinker of a performance that Oscar the Grouch would be proud of. The tournament debutants from an archipelago off the coast of West Africa can now advance to the Round of 32 with a win against Saudi Arabia in their group-stage finale.

Today, a star-studded slate of matches takes the stage while history beckons. Argentina faces off against Austria at 1 p.m. ET in Dallas, where Lionel Messi is set to try to surpass Miroslav Klose’s all-time tournament scoring record with both players tied at 16 goals.

Then, Kylian Mbappé’s France meets Iraq at 5 p.m. ET in Philadelphia before a potential match of the group stage arrives when Norway clashes with Senegal at 8 p.m. ET at the New York New Jersey Stadium. The terrifying freight train of a footballer that is Erling Haaland meets one of Africa’s finest sides in the Lions of Teranga, as veterans Kalidou Koulibaly and Édouard Mendy look to stop the Norwegians in their tracks.

For more World Cup coverage sent straight to your inbox every morning, subscribe to the Men in Blazers newsletter. We’ll be covering every match, every goal and every joyous moment that soccer’s biggest spectacle is sure to bring.
What We’re Watching
World Cup! All the games are on Telemundo and Peacock, and all times are Eastern:
1 p.m.: Argentina vs Austria5 p.m.: France vs. Iraq8 p.m.: Norway vs. Senegal11 p.m.: Jordan vs. Algeria
1 p.m.: Argentina vs Austria
5 p.m.: France vs. Iraq
8 p.m.: Norway vs. Senegal
11 p.m.: Jordan vs. Algeria
That’s it for now! We’ll be back tomorrow.

title: Beyoncé Reveals the Sweet Reason Jay-Z First Got Locs, and It Involves Blue Ivy
summary: Beyoncé shares the story behind Jay-Z’s dreadlocks and why he recently made another dramatic hair transformation.
story: After switching up his look years ago in honor of his and Beyoncé’s oldest child, 14-year-old Blue Ivy Carter, Jay-Z recently made another dramatic hair transformation.

On May 30, the rap superstar surprised fans when he headlined the Roots Picnic in Philadelphia. Following years of sporting lengthy dreadlocks, Jay-Z appeared on stage rocking a full afro. In a YouTube video shared on the account for Cécred, Beyoncé’s hair care line, the singer revealed the significance behind her husband’s latest ‘do and his hair journey over about the last decade.

In between clips of Jay-Z touring Philadelphia and watching a stylist or his wife comb out his locs, Beyoncé explained in a voiceover that her husband wanted to change his hair to celebrate the legacy of his father, Adnis Reeves, because his favorite sports team was from the City of Brotherly Love.
“His dad used to rock a fro, so he wanted to rock a fro in his honor,” she shared.

The superstar added that it wasn’t the first time Jay-Z used his hair to convey a powerful message. She said that her husband had been wearing locs for over eight years to boost Blue Ivy’s confidence.
“I thought it was always beautiful that the real reason Jay grew out his hair was to show Blue that her hair was like his hair,” Beyoncé shared.
The clip then cut to footage of a young Blue Ivy with short and curly hair, smiling and posing in the “Formation” music video and other projects.

“Let me rewind and tell you some backstory. Our daughter was about five, and she wasn’t feeling very confident about her hair at that time,” Beyoncé recalled. “When she told her father that, it inspired Jay to grow out his hair.”
She continued, “We really wanted her to love and embrace every inch of her gorgeous curls.”

Beyoncé said no one predicted how long Jay-Z’s hair would grow. “Who would’ve thunk all of this healthy hair could grow beyond that low-cut Caesar with the deep waves?” she asked, referencing the lyrics to the Destiny’s Child song “Soldier.”

The “Alien Superstar” singer said that her daughter realized at 5 years old that she had the same hair texture as her father. So after eight years of growth, Beyoncé didn’t want her husband to get rid of his locs.
“I was pretty obsessed with Jay’s locks. Honestly, I didn’t really want him to cut them. It’s a lot of memories stored in locs,” she noted, comparing the style to “antennas.”
“Hair grows, people grow, so the transformation can be emotional,” she explained before the rest of the video showed the six-day removal process and an extra day of detangling that led to his afro.
“And on show day, his fro was beautiful, from start to finish,” she proudly concluded. “He is forever ‘The Blueprint.’”
She also titled the video “The Blueprint,” after the rapper’s successful and influential sixth studio album.

title: Meet the Casa Amor Bombshells for ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8
summary: “Love Island USA” Season 8 is making Casa Amor history. Learn everything you need to know about the 18 new islanders. Here’s the cast and their Instagrams.
story: “Love Island USA” Season 8 is making Casa Amor history.

During Episode 17, the moment “Love Island” fans wait for every season — the return of Casa Amor — finally arrived. But this time, the Peacock dating show introduced the bombshells with a twist. While the OG girls prepared to impress their men and raise their heart rates in the classic “Hearts on Fire” challenge, the boys received a text instructing them to sneak out of the villa and head to Casa Amor.

There, the boys participated in the “Hearts on Fire” challenge with the six new bombshells. Meanwhile, the OG girls watched all the shenanigans unfold in real time via a giant screen set up in the main villa, where they heard and saw how eager the boys were to get to know the other ladies.

All six bombshells made their mark, sharing kisses with the boys and showing off their seductive dance moves. After a few minutes, Corbin already declared that he planned to couple up with one of them.

And what about the male Casa Amor bombshells? Well, they won’t be fully revealed until Episode 18, out June 22. However, host Ariana Madix teased another surprise in the episode preview: 12 male bombshells will enter the main villa and flirt with the OG girls all week long.
That means a whopping 18 islanders are turning up the heat this week. Struggling to keep track of all of them? Don’t worry, get to know all the Season 8 Casa Amor bombshells below.

title: Mia Sara Says Making Ferris Bueller ‘Was Not That Good an Experience’
summary: Mia Sara recalls her difficult experience making Ferris Bueller’s Day Off, revealing tensions with director John Hughes and a crush on Matthew Broderick.
story: For decades, “Ferris Bueller’s Day Off” has been celebrated as one of the defining teen comedies of the 1980s. But for actor Mia Sara, the reality behind the scenes was far less idyllic than the film’s enduring reputation might suggest.

Released in 1986, the John Hughes-directed classic follows a wisecracking Chicago teenager who fakes an illness and embarks on an unforgettable day of adventure with his girlfriend and best friend while dodging school officials determined to catch him.

In a conversation with The Times of London, Sara, who starred as Ferris Bueller’s girlfriend Sloane Peterson, revealed that her memories of making the beloved film were complicated, citing tensions with director Hughes and her own struggles as a young performer navigating a major Hollywood production.
Sara has largely stepped away from Hollywood and now focuses on writing. Her website biography reads, “Mia Sara used to be an actress, but recovered her senses and now she writes.”

“I don’t really give interviews because making ‘Ferris Bueller’ was not that good an experience for me,” Sara said. “But I’m very aware of what a precious thing this movie is, and I don’t want to disappoint people. But I didn’t really get along well with John.”
Sara, born Mia Sarapochiello, was only 17 years old when she joined the cast of the 1986 classic.

“John was a strange guy,” she told the outlet. “He wanted us all to hang out together and to introduce us to the French New Wave films. But the others were seasoned actors and I was a snotty New York kid and had seen all those movies, so he was frustrated in that desire.”
“I didn’t have the emotional maturity to deal with other people’s egos, or my own,” Sara added.

Hughes died in 2009 at the age of 59, but his legacy remains firmly intact. Widely regarded as one of the most influential filmmakers of the 1980s, he is beloved by generations of moviegoers for directing and writing iconic films including “The Breakfast Club,” “Sixteen Candles” and“Ferris Bueller’s Day Off.”
More on 'Ferris Bueller'
‘Ferris Bueller’s Day Off’ Stars Reflect on 40th Anniversary of the Classic ComedyNew Book Celebrating ‘Ferris Bueller’s’ 40th Anniversary Gives Fans ‘Deeper Understanding’ of Classic Movie
‘Ferris Bueller’s Day Off’ Stars Reflect on 40th Anniversary of the Classic Comedy
New Book Celebrating ‘Ferris Bueller’s’ 40th Anniversary Gives Fans ‘Deeper Understanding’ of Classic Movie

Despite her frustrations with the production, Sara’s memories of the film were not entirely negative. She also revealed that she developed a crush on her leading man, Matthew Broderick, who played Ferris Bueller.
“Yeah, I had no chance!” Sara admitted to the outlet. “I had a massive on Matthew during filming, but it was very much unrequited.”
Sara, in an 1986 interview with UPI, said it wasn’t “love at first sight” with Hughes (but said it was with director Ridley Scott, with whom she worked on “Legend”).

‘But it was funny. He didn’t know how old I was and said he wanted an older girl to play the 17-year-old. He said it would take someone older to give her the kind of dignity she needed. He almost fell out of his chair when I told him I was only 18.’

title: Princess Charlotte, 11, Poses with Dad William in Never-Before-Seen Photo for His Birthday and Father’s Day
summary: A new social media post features Prince William and Princess Charlotte in a sweet Father’s Day and birthday tribute.
story: How oh so royally sweet!

The Instagram account shared by Prince William and the former Kate Middleton posted a touching photo on Sunday of the dad of three with his daughter Princess Charlotte, 11. The photo was shared in celebration of the prince’s 44th birthday, which also happens to fall on Father’s Day.
In the photo, William — dressed in the uniform of the Welsh Guards of which he serves as the colonel — has his arm around Princess Charlotte, who’s sporting a white dress.

“Happy birthday and Father’s Day to the best Papa in the World! We love you very much. C, G, C & L 💕,” the post said, with the initials referring to Middleton, whose formal title is Catherine, Princess of Wales, Prince George, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis.
The photo drew a lot of love from fans in addition to comments that pointed out the strong resemblance between Prince William and his daughter, the middle child of his three children.
“William really said copy paste,” one Instagram user wrote.
Another fan wrote that “Charlotte really is her Daddy’s twin 🩷🫶.”
The royal family’s official social media account also celebrated the prince’s big day with a photo of William and his own father King Charles.
“Happy Birthday to The Prince of Wales!” the post said.

title: Here’s What Critics Are Saying About ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3
summary: Read critics’ reviews for “House of the Dragon” Season 3, which premiered on June 21.
story: The third season of “House of the Dragon” came out breathing fire with the highly anticipated Battle of the Gullet, but how are critics receiving the show’s return after two years?
The June 21 premiere of the HBO hit featured a bloody, dragon-filled naval battle that seemed to be the antithesis of Season 2, which was dinged by critics and fans for its slow-moving pace.

The show stars Matt Smith, Olivia Cooke and Emma D’Arcy along with this season’s cast of newcomers depicting the latest saga in the ongoing civil war between the Black and Green factions of the Targaryen family. The “Game of Thrones” prequel is based on the George R.R. Martin book “Fire & Blood.”

While it still can be hard for viewers to keep together the sprawling cast of characters (Rhaenyra or Rhaena? Aemond or Aegon?), critics believe this season shows progress after two seasons that divided critics and fans.
Here’s how some major outlets have reviewed the newest season, which will be eight episodes.
Variety

Reviewer Alison Herman calls the Battle of the Gullet in the season premiere “spectacular,” but writes that “the more exciting development in Season 3 is much more intimate in scope than hordes of troops descending into chaos.”
She praised the performances of Cooke and D’Arcy in central scenes between Rhaenyra and Alicent Hightower, but writes that “not all of the show’s connections are so well-realized.”
A character to watch in the new season is Alicent’s cousin, Ormund Hightower (James Norton), who serves as a “chaos agent,” according to Herman.

“Whether they provide surprise and distraction or anchoring ballast, it’s the people who make ‘House of the Dragon’ worth enduring the predetermined devastation,” Herman writes. “The dragons are just the CGI flying lizards on top.”
The Hollywood Reporter

Daniel Fienberg writes that the first two seasons featured “too many similar-looking characters with similar-sounding names and similar versions of ‘complexity’ and, yes, too many darned dragons,” and writes that Season 3 “is, in many ways, the same.”

“The series is still too packed, too narratively rushed and, as much as I’m certain passionate fans will disagree, the surplus of dragons and special effects has become somewhat anticlimactic,” he wrote.
However, he also writes that the third and fourth episodes of Season 3 are his favorite episodes of the whole series thus far.
“Because they were funnier, smarter and a little more intimate in scale, albeit with episode lengths of between 56 and 64 minutes and, yes, lots of dragons,” he wrote.
Fienberg also notes that there are “big deaths” of prominent characters in the first couple of episodes.
He also had praise for the performances of some of the main castmembers.
“D’Arcy has never been better. Smith remains wonderfully hammy,” he wrote.
Fienberg also notes Norton’s performance as Ormund Hightower as one of the best of the cast newcomers.
He then summarized his overall opinion of the first four episodes.

“Anyway, this all boils down to me really liking the third episode, liking portions of the fourth and fearing that no matter how much I like the change of direction that occurs in much of those two episodes, ‘House of the Dragon’ has too much on its plate and too little time to do everything,” he wrote.
USA Today
Kelly Lawler labels the first two seasons of the show “woefully disappointing” with “too many blandly interchangeable characters,” but sees “glimmers of hope” in Season 3.
She writes that the “pace quickens” and features a few more lighter moments amid the gruesome violence.
“Is it enough to light the ‘Thrones’ flame? Certainly not,” she wrote. “But maybe it’s enough to eke something more interesting out of what was previously a sunk-cost-fallacy turned TV show.”
The first four episodes contain “tiny sparks of interest, from some witty dialogue to genuine plot momentum to intriguing new characters popping up,” according to Lawler.
Vulture
Critic Roxana Hadadi sees the new season in a favorable light, writing that the show has “fixed its momentum problem” after the sluggish Season 2.

“It’s bleak as hell, and it’s also the best ‘House of the Dragon’ has ever been, clear-eyed about the story it’s telling and no longer so enamored with the people grasping for power or the dragons they use for their bidding,” she wrote.
Hadadi agrees with The Hollywood Reporter review about the third episode.

“This season’s third episode should be recognized as the best hour of the series so far, a thorough, process-centric portrait of what’s required to lead the commonfolk and sit on the maybe-sentient Iron Throne, which is famed for its ability to accept or reject a ruler,” she wrote.
She also compared parts of this season to the legendary “Game of Thrones,” which ran from 2011 to 2019 on HBO.

“The questions raised about whether any of these characters really earned the power that’s been handed to them, and the realization that all of them, one way or another, will likely meet their end without the honor they thought they were due, propels ‘HOTD’ into somber storytelling heights that haven’t been seen in this franchise since midway through ‘GOT’s’ original run,” she wrote.

title: Meghan Markle Shares Rare Photo of Harry and Their 2 Kids For Father’s Day: ‘Our One And Only’
summary: Meghan Markle pays tribute to her husband Prince Harry on Father’s Day.
story: Meghan Markle has some sweet words to say about her husband Prince Harry.
Markle, whose official title is Duchess of Sussex, shared a photo on Sunday of Harry with the couple’s two children, Prince Archie, 7, and Princess Lilibet, 5, in honor of Father’s Day.
“They’re so lucky to have you,” Markle wrote in the caption. “We all are.”
The photo features Archie and Lilibet, hugging their father tightly as he smiles at the camera.

The couple, who permanently stepped down from their duties as senior members of the British royal family in 2021, have been parenting their children in Montecito, California, away from the frenzy of press and attention in the U.K.

Harry has been open about his experience as a dad and how his own royal background inspired him to parent differently from his father. While on a 2021 episode of the “Armchair Expert” podcast, which is co-hosted by actors Dax Shepard and Monica Padman, Harry shared that the couple’s decision to leave the U.K. had a lot to do with his attempts to “break the cycle” of sometimes-dysfunctional royal parenting.

“There’s a lot of genetic pain and suffering that gets passed on anyway,” Harry said. “As parents we should be doing the most that we can to try to say, ‘You know what, that happened to me, I’m going to make sure it doesn’t happen to you.’”
Harry added that his father, King Charles, likely treated him “the way that he was treated.”
“How can I change that for my own kids?” Harry said on the podcast. “Well, here I am. I’ve now moved my whole family to the U.S.”

Harry isn’t the only royal getting a lot of love this Father’s Day. His older brother Prince William, whose 44th birthday also falls on the holiday, was honored with a photo featuring William and his daughter Princess Charlotte.

“Happy birthday and Father’s Day to the best Papa in the World! We love you very much. C, G, C & L 💕,” the post said, with the initials referring to Middleton, whose formal title is Catherine, Princess of Wales, Prince George, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis.

title: Jay Wheeler on the Profound Impact Fatherhood Has Had on His Life and Career
summary: Jay Wheeler talks about the joy his daughter brings him, how he separates work and fatherhood, as well as parenting advice superstars like Maluma and J Balvin have given him.
story: For recording artist Jay Wheeler, there is “nothing better than being a father.”

Wheeler, 32, and wife Zhamira Zambrano, 28, welcomed their first child together in September 2024. Now, over a year into fatherhood, the artist emphasizes the joy of being a dad and the impact it’s had on his life and career.

“This is the greatest prize, the greatest blessing that God has ever given me. I’m always with her, every single day, every single night, every single morning, afternoon. She’s my best friend,” the Puerto Rican singer tells TODAY.com about his 1-year-old daughter Aiunii. “It’s one of the most beautiful things that I have in my life.”

The three-time Latin Grammy nominee credits his mother and his religious upbringing for maintaining strong family values. He shares that prayer and playing video games with friends helps his mental health.

Much of his feelings around family have made it into his music. Shortly after his baby girl’s birth, he was inspired to write “Aiunii,” a sentimental song about a father’s promise to his daughter. The song is featured on his 2025 album “Girasoles.”

“You came along and changed my story/I wasn’t ready/And even though I’m scared/It feels wonderful,” he sings, later adding, “I cannot promise to be there/For the rest of your life, but I can love you/For the rest of mine.”

In May, he released his latest album, “La Voz Favorita,” which translates to “The Favorite Voice.” He calls it “the most beautiful project I’ve ever done in my life,” in part because of the experiences with his daughter and wife that have helped shape the music.
“I left my heart in that album,” he says.

Ahead of his second Father’s Day with Aiunii, Wheeler shares insight on his everyday life, conversations he and Zhamira had about parenting, his work-life balance and the parenting advice fellow Latin superstars J Balvin and Maluma gave him.
This interview has been edited and condensed for clarity.

My parents, especially my mom, just being a Christian and following God. I remember I told her, “I don’t know how you did it with me, with my brother.” We don’t smoke, we don’t drink, we don’t go out, we weren’t with a lot of girls. We didn’t do that. And she was like, “I just prayed every single night, every single day. I prayed for you guys.”

She was the most amazing mom. I always tell her that I want to be like her. But at the same time, she wasn’t too touchy, (affectionate). She showed us love in 100 different ways, but that was one way that she didn’t because when she was growing up, she didn’t get it as much. Something that I learned is to give hugs, give kisses. There’s a lot of ways to express love, but physically, sometimes it’s even better than anything.

I feel like I’m more strict and I feel like you have to have that because, especially in these times, the world is going crazy. If you let your kids do whatever they want, I think they could get lost in this world. I want my daughter to be whatever she wants to be. At the same time, I want her to be respectful, I want her to have great values, I want her to treat people well.
I always focus on her... saying “thank you, you’re welcome, cómo estás?, un placer,” all of that. She’s 1 year old, but believe it or not, she knows how to do all of those things.

She’s the most beautiful thing that’s ever happened in my life. So imagine keeping that private from the world forever? It was going to be horrible. That’s one (reason). The second reason was that we go out a lot, we like to eat food, we go to Disney. We like to be normal. I don’t want to put a hoodie on her.

I was scared that she would grow up and be like, “Why did you guys hide me?” And she wouldn’t understand why. I prefer her to get mad and be like, “Yo, you guys exposed me a lot.” I would be like, “Baby, you’re my greatest prize.”
Maluma gave me some great advice when he was here at my home and we were streaming. J Balvin also gave me great advice. He has a little kid and I feel like (Rio) has the same attitude as Aiunii.
From Maluma, he said, “Always be present. Try to always be present.” And from J Balvin, he was like, “Don’t listen to the internet because people are going to go crazy no matter what.”
I try to keep it very separate because the music that we do, when it comes to reggaeton, the language can be strong.

The way I see it, when you watch a movie and you see an actor and you see that he’s violent, but in real life he’s not. He’s acting. He’s in a movie. Our music is just like that. It’s acting. I know some people will not agree with me, or be like, “No, you guys inspire (others), you guys better be careful with what you say, what you do.” I’ve been listening to reggaeton my whole life, a whole bunch of “bad” music my whole life, and I have become the best man that I could ever be. I don’t drink, I don’t smoke, and I’ve been listening to drinking and smoking music my whole life.

When you have values and when you have good parents, you don’t need to worry about what any music says. I try to keep it separate, but when it comes to love music, when it comes to romantic music, I get inspired by my wife, by my little daughter.

I wanted to become a better man. I’ve worked a lot on my temper, on my ego, on my joy. What changed in me was, I have a little girl and I know one day she’ll fall in love and I know how we men are when we’re prideful, when we let our ego take control.

I want my daughter to be like, “I don’t deserve this. I have seen my dad, I have seen the way he loves my mom, the way he treats my mom and I’m not going to let anybody treat me the way I’m not supposed to be treated.”

title: Elmo Picks A Side For World Cup After His NBA Finals Controversy: ‘Don’t Make This A Thing’
summary: Elmo declares support for Team USA in the FIFA World Cup after drawing massive backlash for his neutral stance during the NBA Finals.
story: This time around, Elmo isn’t sitting on the fence.

The “Sesame Street” star said in a video shared on social media Friday that he is supporting Team USA amid the FIFA World Cup tournament. The announcement comes just weeks after Elmo wrote on X that he “hopes both teams have fun” during the NBA Finals. The post drew massive backlash from New Yorkers who accused the puppet of forgetting his roots, with the fictional Sesame Street canonically based in the Big Apple.
“Elmo wants Team USA to win. OK? Just to be clear,” said Elmo, sporting a Team USA jersey, before alluding to the drama. “But Elmo loves everybody. Just to be clear. Don’t make this a thing.”
While Elmo declared his loyalty to Team USA, he reiterated that he loves all, including “everybody who’s playing” in the tournament.

It’s not surprising that Elmo would want to clear the air. Elmo, who’s 3 years old, was labeled a “coward” by angry Knicks fans for his failure to support the hometown team. The red furry monster attempted days later to course correct, telling social media users to “KNICKS that last message! Elmo didn’t mean to SPUR you on!” But it was too little too late.
Official city agencies even got in on the Elmo hate, with the NYC Department of Transportation threatening to take down the iconic “Sesame Street” sign on the corner of Broadway and West 63rd Street.
Perhaps the biggest dagger to the heart came from Cookie Monster, who took the opportunity to troll his pal.
“Elmo teach me not to eat trophy. Me teach Elmo to pick a team,” Cookie Monster said on X.

After the Knicks won the Finals, ending a 53-year championship drought, captain Jalen Brunson extended an olive branch. During the parade celebration this past Thursday, Brunson brought with him an Elmo plushie with a sign that read, “Forgive Me PLEASE.”

title: Here’s What Critics Are Saying About ‘Toy Story 5’
summary: What are critics saying about Toy Story 5? Read reviews and why it’s “maybe a perfect ending.”
story: “Toy Story 5” is the latest installment of Pixar’s longest-running franchise, which began in 1995.

The adventures of Buzz, Woody, Jessie, Bo Peep, Mr. Potato Head and the rest of their friends have been the inspiration for spinoffs, like the 2022 movie “Lightyear,” and countless real-life toys. Audiences have grown up alongside Andy and Bonnie.

Now, over 30 years since this story first began and seven years since “Toy Story 4,” “Toy Story 5” is here to address the emerging influence of technology over analog toys, like the “iPad kid” debate — or, in Bonnie’s case, her Lilypad (voiced by Greta Lee).
Keep reading to learn where major outlets stand on how “Toy Story 5” depicts this digital reality facing young kids and how the new movie, out June 19, fares among its four predecessors.
The New York Times
New York Times chief film critic Manohla Dargis says in her review that the latest “Toy Story” installment is about “loneliness and wanting to belong.”
It’s also concerned with themes from the past movies (“play, individuality, obsolescence, consumerism”), but now set in the age of “technocapitalsm.”

For Dargis, one of the most “genuinely poignant” parts of the movie is how the actors, like Tom Hanks and Joan Cusack, have aged along with the characters they are playing: “While time has only lightly scuffed the toys themselves over these many years, you can hear its passing in the voices of the performers.”
Ultimately, though, she feels there’s “only so far” Pixar can “push this story.”
“It’s fine, pretty and amusing, but if no one’s heart seems in it, perhaps it’s time to make way for other toys,” she writes.
The Hollywood Reporter

Even though chief film critic David Rooney believes the franchise “peaked with its magical second and third entries,” he still views “Toy Story 5” as an addition that “does the enduring franchise proud.”
Rooney reflects on why this may be the case, and pinpoints it to one actor and character’s return.

“Perhaps the key gain is that this scenario puts Jessie — national treasure Joan Cusack, returning to features after a quiet stretch of semi-retirement and bringing warmth, plucky spirit and tender lvulnerability to her voiceover work — in charge,” he writes.

Bonnie, who is now caring for Jessie alongside the rest of Andy’s hand-me-down toys, is less inclined to spend time on devices than her neighborhood friends. Bonnie’s parents buy her a Lilypad to get her up to speed socially, and this brings back bad memories for Jessie of being outgrown.
The Hollywood Reporter credits the movie for “heart and conviction” and “the insanely catchy original song from Taylor Swift.
Variety
For Variety’s Owen Gleiberman, “the fifth time’s a charm” with “Toy Story 5.”
He calls the movie “a sublime summing up” and “(just maybe) a perfect ending.”
Gleiberman emphasizes the common theme across the first four films: loss. With “Toy Story 5,” he names “the disappearance of play” as the central battle the movie focuses on.

“What ‘Toy Story 5’ is talking about is what’s known as imaginative play, and that’s not just an activity. It’s a whole dimension, a way for kids to take the universe that’s in their heads and extend it out into the world,” he writes.

“For this is a movie that touches on a profound question: How will kids connect to each other in an era that wants them to grow up too fast by virtualizing themselves?” Gleiberman continues. “The film’s message is: Slow down, be real and play. The fun you take is equal to the fun you make.”
The Guardian’s critic Peter Bradshaw was less charmed.
“As a piece of family-entertainment content it has the unblemished sheen of a brand new smartphone. But at heart, it has gone dead,” Bradshaw writes.

Specifically, Bradshaw said “the jeopardy, the novelty, the ideas and the passion are lacking; the crucial Toy Story theme of mortality feels underpowered, and the film even calamitously loses its nerve with its own big idea.”
While Bradshaw finds the depiction of the toys’ “secret existences” hilarious, the rest of the plot that deviates from this signature structure is “hugely convoluted.”
Bradshaw rounds out his review with the point that even Swift’s soundtrack addition doesn’t save the film from falling victim to “IP exhaustion.”
IndieWire
Contrastingly, David Ehrlich for IndieWire said “the series is only getting sharper with age” with “Toy Story 5,” giving the movie a B+ grade.

Ehrlich praises “Toy Story 5” for, even in “all its flaws,” proving “sharper than any of these movies have been since the late ’90s” and recognizing “how tech poses an unprecedented danger to the nature of play itself — how it threatens to make kids grow up even faster than they already did.”
Like other critics, he was also satisfied by Greta Lee’s debut into the toy franchise with the character of Lilypad.
A character Ehrlich was less thrilled with, however, was one of the leading faces: Woody.

“Woody’s superfluousness to the plot reflects his growing comfort with irrelevance, but that argument would carry more weight if this movie didn’t try so hard to return him to his former stature. Tom Hanks isn’t above a supporting role, but this franchise isn’t totally comfortable with him playing one yet.”

Despite some digs at a “scattered” and “somewhat undercooked” plot, Jessie’s navigation of obsolesce is among some standout moments that prove, for Ehrlich, that “this movie knows that life is never sweeter than it is during the moments, and years, when we simply can’t accept that love is also made out of plastic.”

title: Anne Hathaway Announces Pregnancy With Baby No. 3
summary: Anne Hathaway revealed in an Instagram video that she is pregnant with her third child. She shares two sons with husband Adam Schulman.
story: Anne Hathaway’s family is expanding.
The Academy Award winner, 43, announced she’s pregnant in an Instagram video she posted on June 19.

In the clip, set to Barbara Lewis’ “Baby, I’m Yours,” Hathaway walks into a room wearing a white gown holding her stomach. She then moves her hands to show a baby bump while she smiles and then places her hands on her tummy once again before walking out of the frame.
“x Baby, I’m yours x,” she captioned the video.
A representative for Hathaway has not replied to TODAY.com’s request for comment.
Hathaway and husband Adam Shulman, who married in 2012, are parents to sons Jonathan, 10, and Jack, 6.
While announcing her pregnancy with Jack on Instagram, she alluded to people having trouble conceiving.
“For everyone going through infertility and conception hell, please know it was not a straight line to either of my pregnancies. Sending you extra love,” she wrote, in part.
Hathaway revealed in 2024 that she once had a miscarriage.
“It’s really hard to want something so much and to wonder if you’re doing something wrong,” she told Vanity Fair.
She has been open about her struggles with infertility before her second child was born.
“I think that we have a very one-size-fits-all approach to getting pregnant,” she told the Associated Press in 2019. “You get pregnant and for the majority of cases, this is a really happy time.

“But a lot of people who are trying to get pregnant: That’s not really the story. Or that’s one part of the story. And the steps that lead up to that part of the story are really painful and very isolating and full of self-doubt. And I went through that.”

Hathaway is in the midst of a very busy 2026, which has already seen the release of two films, including “Mother Mary” and the highly anticipated “The Devil Wears Prada 2.” She will also star in another high profile film, “The Odyssey,” which comes out July 17, as well as “The End of Oak Street,” scheduled for an Aug. 14 release.

title: Tay Keith, Grammy-Nominated Record Producer, Found Dead at 29
summary: Grammy-nominated hip-hop record producer Tay Keith has died in his Nashville home, local police confirmed on June 18. No foul play is suspected.
story: Grammy-nominated record producer Tay Keith has died in his Nashville home, local police said on June 18.
No cause of death was given for his death, which was discovered during a welfare check.

“No foul play is suspected in the death of Brytavious Chambers, 29, also known as Grammy nominated record producer Tay Keith,” the police wrote in a post on X. “He was found dead in his Martin St apt this afternoon by officers performing a welfare check. His death is unclassified pending autopsy results.”

The Tennessee native worked with an impressive roster of talent during his career, including Beyoncé and Drake. He produced four No. 1 hits and 11 top 10 songs on the Billboard Hot 100, including Travis Scott’s 2018 single “Sicko Mode,” which he worked on while a student at Middle Tennessee State University, as well as Drake’s 2023 hit “First Person Shooter.”
“Sicko Mode” earned a Grammy nomination for Keith in 2019. He earned another Grammy nomination in 2024 for the Drake-21 Savage collaboration, “Rich Flex.”

Keith has the most No. 1s on the Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart this decade, with six, according to Billboard. He also helped launch the career of Sexyy Red, working with her multiple times, including on the tracks “Get It Sexxy” and “Pound Town.”

title: Jelly Roll, Bunnie XO Comment Separately on Their Divorce: ‘We’re Still Having a Baby Together’
summary: Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO separately commented about their divorce. She said they still plan to have a baby together, and he said there was no cheating involved.
story: Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO are speaking out separately about their divorce — as well as the baby they are planning to welcome.

Bunnie XO, whose real name is Alisa DeFord, revealed on the June 18 episode of her “Dumb Blonde Podcast” that she and Jelly Roll, whose real name is Jason DeFord, are having a baby together, while confirming they have split.

“And you guys are going to be shocked to hear this, but we’re still having a baby. We’re still having a baby, guys. We’re still having a baby together,” she said. “So, we have been the most unconventional couple that you guys have ever encountered.”
She said they lost four embryos in the course of their relationship and they both plan to raise the baby.

“We’re still having a baby together. We’re going to co-parent together,” she said. “Jay is my best friend. Like, this isn’t what you guys think this is. Nobody cheated on the other person. It’s literally just we served our purpose for each other. And I’m excited to discover myself single, and single and sober, which is crazy.”
The couple married in 2016. Bunnie XO revealed that the “Need a Favor” singer has started dating, but didn’t provide more context.
“I’m not going to say anything about Jay’s actions because I don’t feel like it’s the right time and I don’t feel like it’s my place,” she said.
“Jay and I have been together going on 10 years and he is my best friend. He has always been my best friend and I love that man more than life itself,” she added.
Bunnie XO also she was not in favor of the breakup, adding the divorce “was not mutual” and she gave the green light for their split due to emotions.

“Even though I told him to file the divorce papers, I was speaking out of anger and just frustration. But was it necessary for us to have a wakeup call and to actually start having these real conversations? Absolutely,” she said.
The three-time Grammy winner, who revealed in 2025 he had cheated on his wife, also spoke about the divorce during a June 18 concert in Saratoga Springs, New York.

“I wasn’t going to talk about this tonight, but while we’re talking about liars, the internet is a liar, too,” he said in remarks he posted on his Instagram. “It’s the only time, and the only city I’m gonna speak about this, so break your camera phones out now. Me and my wife are best friends. We will always be best friends,” he continued as the crowd erupted in cheers.
He then said the couple’s relationship is solid.

“We just got off the phone earlier today. Nobody cheated on nobody. She just did a whole podcast about it. You can go watch it. Every word of it is the truth. That will be my best friend forever. This is the only time I will ever speak about it. Bunnie, I love you, baby,” he said as the crowd once again screamed.
“Thank you for those 10 years. They were incredible. Thank you for the next 10 years of friendship. And 20 beyond that. With that being said, cheers everybody. Thanks for coming out tonight.”

Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO don’t have kids together, but he is father to 18-year-old daughter named Bailee Ann and 9-year-old son named Noah Buddy. Bunnie XO has been open about her desire to have a baby with the singer.

“Now that we’re finally stable financially and somewhat emotionally after all the healing we’ve done over this decade together, we’re talking about the future — ­including growing our family,” she wrote at the time in her memoir, “Stripped Down: Unfiltered and Unapologetic,” People reported in February. “J and I have a surrogate, the sweetest woman ever, and soon I’ll be starting my IVF stims.”
In 2024, Bunnie XO revealed she and Jelly Roll had separated in 2018.

“Who knew that us breaking up in 2018, me moving back to Vegas & you coming to get me back — would have put us on this wild journey called life,” she wrote in a TikTok video. “We finally committed to each other & did everything we promised each other the first night in 2016. Our castle in the sand had to crumble so we could rebuild on solid ground I yuh you so mushhh.”

title: Deidre Hall ‘Slaps’ Craig Melvin on TODAY, ‘Days of Our Lives’-Style
summary: “Days of Our Lives” star Deidre Hall stage-slapped Craig Melvin, at his request, during an appearance on TODAY.
story: Craig Melvin got the soap opera treatment when “Days of Our Lives” star Deidre Hall stopped by TODAY on June 19.
The actor, who is celebrating her 50th year playing Marlena Evans on the iconic soap opera, stage-slapped Craig during their interview — at his request!
“What I’ve always enjoyed is your ability to slap. You are a great slapper,” Craig said. “Have you calculated how many times you’ve slapped?”
“(I) couldn’t even begin to imagine,” Hall said before a montage of some of her memorable slaps played.
Craig then shot his shot to become the next in a long line of victims.
“It would be one of the greatest professional honors of my life if you would slap me,” he said.
“I’d be privileged,” Hall said, prompting Craig to laugh.
They both stood up as they got ready for the big moment.
“I braced my face,” Craig said while they lined up where the cameras were that would capture the moment.
“Be gentle now,” he implored her.

“I’m going to draw my hand back,” she said, as she pulled her right arm behind her head. “And I’m going to come very close to your face. And when you feel that happening, you’re going to snap your head.”
She then launched into the slap and Craig moved his head back just as her hand was about to hit it, placing his hand over his cheek as if he really had been hit, all while a slap sound effect played.
“You OK?” she asked, while he pretended to cry.
“I’m sorry. I meant to miss,” she joked, as Craig laughed.
“You’re the best,” he said.
Hall also opened up Ryan Gosling’s comments earlier this year about how Hall’s work on the show inspired him as an actor, which resulted in her inviting him to the “Days of Our Lives” set.

“I was stunned,” she said about Gosling’s comments. “His work is so impeccable, and he’s so honest and so clean in his work that I thought, ‘Oh my gosh. What is he seeing? I don’t want to let him down.’ But, what a lovely gesture, and I’m beyond flattered.”
As for her future on “Days of Our Lives,” which streams on Peacock, Hall says she has no plans to step away anytime soon.
“As long as my key card opens the gate, I’m there,” she said. “It’s my family. I love that place. I couldn’t resist.”
(Peacock is part of our parent company, NBCUniversal.)

title: Obama Presidential Center Opening: See All the Stars in Attendance
summary: Many stars were in attendance at the Obama Presidential Center opening in Chicago, including Oprah Winfrey, Bruce Springsteen, Tom Hanks and Stephen Colbert.
story: There will be plenty of people in attendance when Barack Obama opens his Obama Presidential Center.

The center, which is located in Chicago and received about $850 million in private funding (including a donation from NBC News’ parent company, Comcast), will hold its grand opening weekend from June 19 to June 21.

“The goal for me has been to create something that’s a living monument not to me, but to this idea of American citizenship,” the former president told TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager in May. “And if we can encourage that, then I’ll feel pretty good, and I think people will really enjoy being there.”
The grand opening ceremony actually took place June 18 and featured plenty of big names, including former presidents Joe Biden, George W. Bush and Bill Clinton.

Obama’s wife, Michelle Obama, has a special bond with Bush, initially captured in 2018 when he gave a mint to the former first lady at John McCain’s funeral, in a moment that went viral and became a tradition for the two. At the center’s opening, the pair honored that moment by posing with a box of Altoids.
Malia and Sasha Obama, now 27 and 25, made a rare joint appearance more than a decade after moving into the White House, when they were 10 and 7.
Here’s a look at who performed at the center’s opening:
The Roots

Here’s a look at who will perform during the opening weekend events:
June 19
Lizzie G will serve as host for all four hours and perform with DJ Shani throughout the day. Lizzie G will also be joined at one point by DJ Duane Powell.
There will be a short Ayodele drum and dance performance, while guests can also learn West African dance steps and play drums.
The South Shore Drill Team, which is based in Chicago and aims to promote education and eliminate violence, will perform.
June 20
Lizzie G returns to host the day’s festivities and will be joined throughout the day at different points by DJ Ca$h Era and DJ Mike P.

The Black Hawk Performance Company will take the stage to highlight their mission of seeking “to honor the ground we dance on, Mother Earth and the many tribes who have made Illinois their home,” according to its website.
The Mandala South Asian Performance Company will play music, dance and put on an interactive performance to support the interaction of multiple cultures.
The rock and roll marching band Mucca Pazza will perform in an homage to marching band and street theater.
June 21
Lizzie G will once again host the day’s events, as well as perform with DJ Rae Chardonnay and then DJ TRQPITECA.
Meagan McNeal + Band will perform, sharing the vocalist’s gospel, jazz and R&B influences.
The day wraps up with a performance by two-time Latin Grammy-winning band Mariachi Herencia de Mexico.

title: George W. Bush and Michelle Obama Have Sweet Reunion, Complete With a Mint Moment
summary: George W. Bush and Michelle Obama playfully paid homage to their 2018 viral mint moment at the opening of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago.
story: George W. Bush and Michelle Obama paid homage to their 2018 viral moment when the two attended the opening of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday, June 18.

After more than a decade of planning and construction, Barack Obama, Michelle Obama and their daughters, Malia and Sasha Obama, were on site to see the Obama Presidential Center finally open to the public. Also in attendance were all the former living presidents and first ladies, excluding President Donald Trump and Melania Trump. At the event, Bush caught up with friend Michelle Obama and the two almost re-created their funny moment in 2018 when he handed her a mint at John McCain’s funeral.

This time, the duo smiled and posed together as Michelle Obama held a tin box of Altoids. Photographers captured the sweet interaction between the two, as well as a group snap of the Obamas, Bushes, Clintons and Bidens posing together.

In April, Bush opened up about his friendship with Michelle Obama in a TODAY interview with daughter Jenna Bush Hager to celebrate the nation’s upcoming 250th anniversary. During their talk, he recalled the exchange the two had at McCain’s funeral.
“I get a little antsy, as I’m sure you know. And I was sitting next to Michelle, that’s who I sit next to at funerals,” he told Jenna.
Bush continued, “I was kinda teasing her and stuff, and I slipped her an Altoid. Not as a joke, but I thought she might want one.”
He remembered after they left the service, Jenna informed him in the car that the moment was trending.
“I didn’t know what trending meant,” he quipped.
Bush then explained why he thought the interaction went viral.

“It turns out the country is starved to see a white center right Republican and an African-American center left Democrat having fun, and being able to converse, not as political figures, but as citizens,” he shared.
Michelle Obama also spoke about the moment in 2018 when she was promoting her memoir, “Becoming.”
“He has the presence of mind and the sense of humor to bring me a mint, and he made it a point to give me that mint right then and there and that’s the beauty of George Bush,” she praised.

She further gushed about their friendship in a TODAY interview with Jenna in December 2019. Michelle Obama said that whenever she and Bush reunite they catch up on stories about their children and parents.
“Our values are the same. We disagree on policy, but we don’t disagree on humanity. We don’t disagree about love and compassion,” she added.

title: Sean Reifel on How Being A Parent Impacted His ‘Love Island USA’ Experience
summary: Sean Reifel from “Love Island USA” Season 8 talks about parenting, being dumped and the couple viewers may be wrong about.
story: For Sean Reifel, every decision comes back to one person: his son.

The “Love Island USA” Season 8 islander entered the villa with a different perspective than many of his fellow islanders. Whether discussing relationships, career choices or the decision to put his life on national television, Sean tells TODAY.com he leads with the mindset that life is too short not to take chances.

During his time in Fiji, Sean “explored” multiple “connections” — “Love Island” lingo for looking for a potential relationship — but as the show premiered, much of the conversation surrounding him wasn’t about his love life.

Instead, online discussions centered on Sean’s life outside the show. The mayor of Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, publicly criticized Sean for resigning from his job as a police officer for the reality dating show, calling it “disappointing.” (Sean clarifies to TODAY.com he did not resign.) But now as the first contestant dumped from the island, some have wondered whether appearing on “Love Island USA” was a risk worth taking.
Speaking with TODAY.com, Sean discusses fatherhood, taking chances, the realities of life inside the villa and the couple he thinks deserves more credit.
This interview has been edited for clarity.

I think it would be my personality in full. I saw a lot of comments saying that I was reserved and stuff like that. I’m an absolute goofball when I get comfortable. I’ll be so honest, it was just  nerve-racking at first to be on the show, but I love making jokes. I love dancing and singing, even though I’m not good at either of them. So I wouldn’t say that I was reserved, but I think my personality can come off that way. A lot of people were saying I didn’t pull many (people) for chats and things of that nature, but I did. I pulled a lot of people for chats. It’s just that you condense 24 hours into one hour, there’s only so much that you can put in there.

When I did the little introduction video on social media (after leaving the villa), people were laughing in shock. They didn’t know I read books and that I made clay animation and stuff, which I don’t do all the time. It was just a side quest where I was like, oh, I think this would be fun to do today.

I think being a dad definitely changes my outlook on relationships and how I approach them, because the most important thing is that my son already has a phenomenal mother. I’m not looking for someone to mother him. But with having a son, I’m very particular on the example I set for him growing up. When I had my son, I wanted to show him the example of how to be in a very loving and committed relationship and have that last long-term. So when he gets to that point in life, he can see that I’m doing it, too, and I’m setting the example, and he’s comfortable sitting down with me and having that conversation. Like, “Dad, I really like this girl and I want something serious,” he can tell me about it. And I can give him advice there, too, rather than if I’m out here running around doing whatever I want, being with whoever I want. I don’t want to set that example for him.

You know, if he’s young and he wants to goof off and do stuff, he can get that advice from his friends. But when it’s time to do grown man things and, you know, really take that step in life to have something that’s worthwhile, I want to set that example for him.

I just think that having a son completely changed my approach to dating and looking to the future and also how I treat people and the kindness that I show. I just want to really set that example for him.

The first thing would be: Don’t ever be too cool to try. You know, it’s easy to look at something like “Love Island” and say, oh, why would you do that? But at the end of the day, I think that things such as love in relationships are worth doing wild things for and putting yourself out there and not being afraid of the backlash you get from it.

Right now my son is young. He’s going to listen to whatever I say. But when he gets in his preteen years, he’s going to be like, “I know better. I’m not listening to anything you say.” And then as he becomes an adult and gets into life, he’s gonna look for my advice, if my life also shows proof that I’ve done that. So when he’s gonna do something scary, or he’s gonna take that chance and jump off the cliff and see if he flies or doesn’t fly, he comes to me and I can give him advice and say, “Hey, I also did this experience, and I took a chance on it and I put myself out there.”

When I tell him to do that, and I tell him it’s worth it to see and that life’s journey is about what you do, all that stuff is beautiful, if you try, then I can point to that and show him. And then also, he can make fun of me, too, about it and we can have some good laughs. Because in my head, I’m saying, you know, he can’t watch until he’s 18. But as you said, I know it’s going to come up when he’s younger and kids in school will probably tell him about it. He might watch it when he’s 12 and not tell me or something.

So I did not resign from my job, actually. I told them where I was going, and they told me to put in a request for time off. They approved my vacation in PTO, but it was gonna run out because I was a newer employee, so I asked for unpaid leave to, you know, finish my contract on the back end. They said they’ll submit it.
They let me know while I was commuting to the island that, hey, we’re gonna accept your vacation PTO, but once your time runs out, we won’t do unpaid leave. So I didn’t resign from it.

But I think, again, how do I put this? To me, life’s journey is very important, and having this opportunity that I would never have in any other time in my life — I’m not a model, not an actor, not a professional athlete — I just wouldn’t have had this experience in life. At the end of the day, I think that in my experience, through law enforcement I’ve been led to uncomfortable truths about how quickly life can go. And how at the end of your life, all you have is the memories and the chances you took and the things you did.

For me, it was this max six-weeks experience, and then I can still go and do law enforcement, and I can still go and do the things I’ve done before. And, for me, it just came down to, whether I’m on my deathbed at 80 or whether life abruptly is taken from me without me being prepared for it, I wanted to sit and know that I tried, and I did what I could and have the experiences I could.
So looking back on it, I would make the same decision because honestly, I think that what’s life is, if you’re not taking chances, you know, especially on love. Love is cool as f---.

So the chats you guys see are, you have to condense them, obviously, but you also have to understand that all of us islanders are having chats throughout the day. Some chats I’ve had were like an hour and a half long. And you end up doing that hour and a half and then it’s lunchtime or something, and then you’re getting ready for the night. So there’s times where you have a challenge midday where you don’t know it’s coming because you’re just waiting on the text. So you’re thinking, oh, you know, maybe I’ll get a quick workout in after I have breakfast, and then you hear that, you know, forbidden sound. And then quickly, now you have to get ready and have the day, but you don’t have clocks and you don’t know what’s coming. You have that situation where, oh, I thought I was going to have time to talk to you, but, you know, I had a ping pong game, or I was working out and I was thinking, oh, I could get to it in a second and then you end up running out of time.

I would say I’ve seen some negative things about Bryce and Trinity. I’m not fully caught up, but I think that they have an incredible connection. I don’t know what maybe viewers saw in recent episodes that would make you think differently, but I think Bryce is a very emotionally mature person. And I think him and Trinity, through what I saw in my time there, were able to have great communication skills. And when you’re looking for communication in a relationship, one of the most important things is how you communicate when you’re upset and things aren’t going well. And I feel that both of them, in my time there seeing them, had a really good ability to be like, “Hey, I’m upset, but I can articulate it very well.” And then they were able to find common ground.

title: All the Stars Who Came Out for the Knicks Parade in New York City
summary: New York City celebrates the Knicks in a historic parade. See all the celebs who showed up including Timothee Chalamet, Ben Stiller, Martha Stewart and more.
story: The New York Knicks overcame a 53-year drought on June 13 when they beat the San Antonio Spurs and took the NBA title, finally bringing home the Larry O’Brien Championship Trophy.

Cheering on their beloved team, New Yorkers poured into the streets of the city to commemorate the magical moment — and the celebration hasn’t stopped. Ecstatic fans get a chance to give the Knicks their flowers on June 18 in a ticker-tape parade that’s one for the ages.

Among the millions of fans lining the streets of lower Manhattan, are some of the Knicks’ biggest celeb supporters including Ben Stiller, Timothée Chalamet, Chris Rock, Spike Lee, Martha Stewart and many others. Read on to see who’s on location to celebrate the Knicks in this history-making parade.
Ben Stiller

One of the Knicks’ biggest die-hard fans is actor and director Ben Stiller. Born and raised in New York City, Stiller has been courtside for countless games and is currently working on a docuseries on his favorite NBA team in tandem with HBO and A24. Of the Knicks championship win, Stiller posted on X saying, “It’s a beautiful day in New York and Knicks are NBC Champions and I’m watching our guys do all the shows and life is good today.”
Timothée Chalamet

Often spotted front and center at Madison Square Garden, Oscar nominee Timothée Chalamet has been a Knicks fan through thick and thin. The “Dune” actor was on hand in San Antonio when the Knicks took the championship and notably shouted in front of ESPN cameras, “Way rather this than the Oscars! Come on, baby!”
Chris Rock

A diehard fan, comedian Chris Rock joined along the parade route saying, “This is the most amazing day of my life,” on TODAY’s Threads account. “It’s this, then the birth of my children,” he joked. The “Madagascar” star is a staple at Knicks games and was spotted with Adam Sandler at Game 4 of the championships.
Spike Lee

Undeniably the biggest Knicks fan of them all, Spike Lee was naturally front and center for the championship parade to cheer on his winning team. A season ticket holder since 1985, Lee told MSG Networks during an interview that he’d never been to a parade before saying, “I’m glad it’s this one!”
Martha Stewart

A showstopper in her own right, Martha Stewart has been a Knicks fan for decades. The 84-year-old even had her toe broken while sitting courtside in a 2025 game between the Knicks and the Indiana Pacers. On an episode of “The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon,” the lifestyle mogel revealed that she unwisely wore open-toed shoes to the game and suffered a Bruson fracture after Knicks guard Jalen Brunson accidentally came “crashing down” on her toe.
Mariska Hargitay

To know Mariska Hargitay is to know that the “Law & Order: SVU” star is the Knicks No. 1 fan. A true face-painter at heart, the actor joked to The Hollywood Reporter that the Knicks comeback in Game 4 of the NBA Finals upstaged even her wedding night: “I love my husband, and our wedding night was great and all, but I think it might have been the greatest night of my life.” Hargitay also called the win one of “the best moments in sports,” telling THR in a statement, “OH MY GOD, I LOVE THIS TEAM!!!”
Tracy Morgan

One of the Knicks most emotional fans is “30 Rock” alum Tracy Morgan. The comedian has notably been spotted in tears at more than one game. Upon winning the championship against the Spurs, Morgan could be seen crying on the court. In an interview with NBA TV, Morgan excitedly said, “Boom!’ That’s all it is. You don’t feel nothing. It’s instant. It’s ‘Boom!’”
Fat Joe

Fat Joe, one of hip hop’s most legendary artists, is one of the Knicks biggest superfans. During a June 9 interview with Variety, the rapper said, “It meant everything to me to watch the Knicks win the championship. I’ve been going to games since I was a kid sitting in the nosebleeds, so it still feels like a dream to have been part of this ride.”

title: Carley Fortune Details the ‘Long’ Call She Had with Meghan Markle About ‘Meet Me at the Lake’ Adaptation
summary: Carley Fortune tells Jenna about where the adaptation for Meet Me at the Lake stands and her relationship with Meghan Markle.
story: Carley Fortune’s romance novels are hitting the screen. “Every Year After,” the Amazon Prime adaptation of her debut “Every Summer After,” premiered in June, and there are more to come.
During the June 18 episode of “Open Book with Jenna,” Fortune gave some details about where the adaptation for “Meet Me at the Lake,” her second novel, stands.

Back in August 2023, news broke that Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, had purchased the rights to the book for an eventual Netflix adaptation through their production company, Archewell Productions. Fortune confirmed the news to The Independent.
“It is so wild,” Fortune, 42, admitted to Jenna about working with the Duke and Duchess of Sussex.
Granting Archewell the rights to the novel wasn’t based on buzz surrounding the royal couple. Rather, it was about a shared understanding of the story’s core, the author said.
Fortune said she signed over the rights to Archewell  after a “really good, long call” with Markle and Archewell film producer Tracy Ryerson.

“I felt like they really understood the story and what mattered about the story,” Fortune said. “It’s a romance, but it’s very much about a mother and daughter, too, and we talked a lot about parenthood, and also about how these love stories are about so much more. It’s a story about grief, it’s a story about mental health, and so I felt like it was in good hands with them.”
More from Carley Fortune
Carley Fortune Wants Her Teenage Self To Feel Seen By ‘Every Year After’How ‘Every Year After’ Handles the Romance Genre’s Most Controversial Plot Twist
Carley Fortune Wants Her Teenage Self To Feel Seen By ‘Every Year After’
How ‘Every Year After’ Handles the Romance Genre’s Most Controversial Plot Twist
She said the project is “still in development.” A director is attached but the script is “still being revised.”
Fortune said her relationship with the royal couple remains exclusively professional. “No, I am not hanging out with Harry and Meghan on weekends,” Fortune joked.
“You can, you could!” Jenna replied.

“Meet Me at the Lake” follows strangers Fern Brookbanks and Will Baxter as they are sent on “a daylong adventure where they make a promise one keeps and the other breaks, with life-changing effects,” per Fortune’s website.

Among the mental health topics Fortune is entrusting the production team to convey is postpartum obsessive-compulsive disorder, or postpartum OCD, which the author herself experienced, she told Jenna.

“What I have found through books is I can weave in things that are really important to me and people engage with them because they are reading this romance, they know it’s going to have a happy ending,” Fortune said. “But they are learning about mental health.”
Of postpartum OCD, she added that the symptoms are “very very hard to talk about” — but she tackles it in the novel anyway.

“A lot of people don’t know about it, I didn’t know about it, and I’ve found it’s been so wonderful to have heard from readers who didn’t know what they were going through until they read the book, and who found courage to talk to a loved one or a doctor,” she said. “I think there’s just such power in that … It’s been a way that I can connect with people.”

title: Olivia Wilde on the Moment She Realized Her Relationship with Jason Sudeikis Was Over
summary: Olivia Wilde is opening up about the conversation that led to her split from ex-fiancé Jason Sudeikis.
story: Olivia Wilde is opening up about the conversation that led to her split from ex-fiancé Jason Sudeikis.

The 42-year-old actor and director was a guest on the June 17 episode of the “Call Her Daddy” podcast and opened up about her past relationships, including with the “Ted Lasso” star, 50. Wilde and Sudeikis, who share 12-year-old son Otis and 9-year-old daughter Daisy, started dating in 2011 and got engaged in 2013. The two called it quits in November 2020.

During her chat with host Alex Cooper, Wilde said that her upcoming movie “The Invite,” which she directs and also stars in, focuses on a couple coming to the realization that they’ve grown apart — a challenging moment she experienced with Sudeikis.

“There was never any public understanding, of course, because they’re not a part of our private lives, but of that breakup,” she said about calling off their engagement. “It’s no surprise to me that I ended up making a movie about relationships and the complexity of determining whether a relationship is over, because it is not an overnight process. It’s very difficult.”
She added that her film, which hits theaters June 26, is centered on “the idea that relationships can come to a place where you become strangers.”

Wilde then recalled the exact date and moment she realized it was time to end her relationship with Sudeikis. The “Don’t Worry Darling” director remembered it being her birthday on March 10, 2020, and attending a party with her then-fiancé.
She shared that the two “had been having a rough time there for a while.”
“We had a real bumpy, bumpy ride,” she added before going into detail about the pivotal conversation.
During the drive home from the birthday party her friends threw, Wilde asked Sudeikis if he bought her a present.
“He said, ‘What would I get you Olivia? I don’t know you,’” she told Cooper. “And he wasn’t wrong. We didn’t know each other anymore.”

Wilde said the interaction was one of the inspirations behind her movie because she wanted to explore the idea of two people getting to a place in their relationship where they stopped being curious about each other.
“That was when we realized it was over, and it was f------ tough. It brought us to the place of, like, ‘OK, this is done. We’re going to end this,’” she continued.
Wilde said that two days later the exes had to go into lockdown with each other when the COVID-19 pandemic began.
Sudeikis previously discussed their split in a 2021 interview with GQ where he said he wasn’t sure why they broke up.

“I’ll have a better understanding of why in a year,” he said at the time. “And an even better one in two, and an even greater one in five, and it’ll go from being, you know, a book of my life to becoming a chapter to a paragraph to a line to a word to a doodle.”
He also said he needed to process the experience and learn from it.
“You take some responsibility for it, hold yourself accountable for what you do, but then also endeavor to learn something beyond the obvious from it,” Sudeikis shared.

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Star Beatriz Reveals How She Feels Watching Gabriel Move On With Jen
summary: “Love Island USA” Season 8 star Beatriz Hatz’s reveals to TODAY.com if there is any “beef” with Melanie and how she feels about Gabriel moving on with Jen.
story: “Love Island USA” Season 8 star Beatriz Hatz’s time in the villa was short but meaningful.

The 25-year-old Paralympian from San Diego was an OG islander this season who entertained fans with her decision on the first episode to explore a connection with Gabriel Vasconcelos over Sean Reifel, her blunt conversations with Melanie Moreno, and her jokes about her limb difference.

But in Episode 9, Beatriz’s time in the villa ended abruptly after America voted to partner Gabriel with new bombshell Jen Terry, leaving the athlete single and vulnerable. She was dumped from the island after Corbin Mims chose to couple up with Melanie and shared a tearful goodbye with Gabriel.
Since then, Beatriz tells TODAY.com that she has been following all the drama that has unfolded with the islanders after her departure, and what fans have been saying about the cast on social media.

Below, Beatriz opens up about her experience on the show, revealing if there is any “beef” between her and Melanie after their argument and how she felt seeing Gabriel move on so quickly. She also shares her thoughts on being a representation of people with disabilities, how she thinks the islanders will behave when Casa Amor starts next week and which islander is misunderstood.
This interview has been edited and condensed for clarity.

It was so cool when I got cast. I was like, “Oh my gosh, no way! This is insane.” They reassured me that, like, “Hey, we picked you because we like you, not because you’re disabled.” But these two things are amazing: I get to be on the show, and I also get to be the representation. There’s a lot of stigmas around disability that I’m just happy that I was there to break these stereotypes.

I think I was able to talk a bit more with the girls about that, and they were able to ask me questions. It was really cool to share this knowledge. Then with the boys, they were so interested in the running blade. It was like a car. They were like, “Oh, what is this? What does it feel like? How heavy is it?” I’m sitting there, show and tell, like, “Hey, do you want to hold it? Do you want to hold the prosthetic? You can.” I felt so much love from my fellow cast members and it was really cool that everybody was so supportive about it.

I’ve been following the show. I’ve been watching the current episodes. I need to go back and watch from the beginning because I want to see how I’m portrayed, what was going on, all the things that were happening that I didn’t particularly know about. I’m curious.

I think I’ve been seeing everybody call Corbin AI. OK, no, he’s a person. He’s multifaceted. He’s got different layers, and maybe we’re just not seeing all of them. I think that’s a misconception. I think everybody has different sides of themselves. Whether or not you’re willing to show that to everybody or just maybe a select few of people, that’s a different thing.

I think two things can be true at once. I think he may still be playing the game. But also I do think that he’s an individual who will probably have different layers, and maybe I just didn’t get to know those sides of him.

We’ve actually told all the girls and all the people in there, like, “Hey, no matter what, when we get out of here, we’re the ones that know what actually happened in here. I know that there’s going to be things that we can’t talk about, but we know what happened. So that’s the only thing that matters.” There’s no one-sided beef, there’s no beef at all. I think when you have friends around you, your friends are able to tell you, “Hey, I actually don’t like this.’ Or, ‘I do like this.’ Or, ‘Can you help me with this? Can you help me with that?” I think that’s just different levels of friendships that maybe people aren’t used to seeing, or maybe they’re not used to seeing that communication style. Like I said, we made up. I shared something very deep. She did as well. You continuously develop those friendships. And what makes a friendship is if you can have a discussion and be open and (say), “OK, I’m willing to take responsibility for this and this.” And then, OK, squash (it). We are now growing our connection as friends. That deepens the friendship.

I think we all have our people that we gravitate the most towards and hang out the most with. I think Melanie and I were really able to relate when we speak Spanish, what our cultural similarities are. That was something that was very deep.
